title: Torktarak

The '''''Torktarak''''' was a [[Braha'tok-class gunship/Legends|''Braha'tok''-class gunship]] under the command of the [[Dornean/Legends|Dornean]] [[Etahn A'baht]] in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dornea/Legends|Dornea's]] [[Dornean Navy|navy]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Dornea loaned the ''Torktarak'', along with its sister [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Braha'tok]]'', to the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Alliance Fleet]], which needed the ships for its upcoming [[Battle of Endor/Legends|assault]] on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]]—an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] capable of destroying entire planets—above the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]].

During the battle, the ''Torktarak'' helped the ''Braha'tok'' fight off the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruiser]] [[Eminence (Carrack-class)|''Eminence'']], a ship more than three times the size of the ''Torktarak''. The two [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] were able to destroy the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|war vessel]], and, after the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] defeated the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]] at Endor and destroyed the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], the relatively unscathed ''Torktarak'' was returned to the Dornean Navy.

==Description==
Built by the [[Dornean Braha'ket Fleetworks Conglomerate/Legends|Dornean Braha'ket Fleetworks Conglomerate]], the ''Torktarak'' was a [[Braha'tok-class gunship/Legends|''Braha'tok''-class gunship]] in the [[Dornean Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. The ''Torktarak'' was ninety meters long and equipped with eight double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and [[Concussion missile launcher/Legends|concussion missile launchers]] each. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] came equipped with [[Targeting computer/Legends|fire control]], maneuvering, and command capabilities, all of which were centralized in the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. The ''Torktarak'' also possessed limited [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] space and lacked ground [[Troop/Legends|troop]] complement.<ref name="db" /> The ''Torktarak'' also possessed [[Engine/Legends|engines]] that came equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]].<ref name="rotj" />

==History==
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] asked the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dornea/Legends|Dornea]]—which had been fighting its own [[Dornea's war against the Empire|private war]] against the Empire for [[Standard year/Legends|years]]—for [[Battleship/Legends|battleships]] to assist in their upcoming [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]],<ref name="db" /> an Imperial [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] that could destroy an entire [[Planet/Legends|planet]], still under construction over the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]].<ref name="rotj" /> The [[Dornean/Legends|Dorneans]] decided to send the ''Torktarak'', along with its sister [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Braha'tok]]'', under the command of [[Etahn A'baht]] and [[Kiles L'toth]], respectively. Since the ''Braha'tok''-class [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] had not been outside of the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] surrounding Dornea before 4 ABY, and to avoid arousing the suspicion from [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], the Dornean government claimed that the two ships were undergoing a "shakedown cruise."<ref name="db" />

[[File:BrahatokClassGunship-Databank.jpg|thumb|left|270px|A ''Braha'tok''-class gunship]]
The ''Torktarak'' and the ''Braha'tok'' met up with the Alliance Fleet, under the command of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the planet of [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. Both of the ''Braha'tok''-class gunships left with the Alliance Fleet for the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]].<ref name="db" /> However, when the Alliance Fleet came out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] over Endor, they were caught in a trap devised by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The Alliance discovered that the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] protecting the second [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]—which was supposed to have been taken down by a [[Endor strike team/Legends|strike team]] that had already landed on Endor—was still up. Additionally, the Alliance was met by a large [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]] under the command of Admiral [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]], trapping the Rebels in the [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="rotj" />

The ''Torktarak'', the ''Braha'tok'', and the rest of the Alliance Fleet engaged the Imperials, hoping the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator]] on Endor below would be taken down by the ground strike team before the Imperial Fleet defeated them.<ref name="rotj" /> During the [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]], the ''Torktarak'' was forced to go to the aid of the ''Braha'tok'', which had engaged the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''–class cruiser]] [[Eminence (Carrack-class)|''Eminence'']], a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] that was more than three times the size of the Dornean gunships. Together, the ''Braha'tok'' and the ''Torktarak'' were able to destroy the ''Eminence'' after the ''Torktarak'' attacked the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warship]] from behind. However, the ''Braha'tok'' was seriously damaged during the engagement.<ref name="db" />

After the ''Eminence'' was destroyed, the ''Torktarak'' continued to provide cover for the damaged ''Braha'tok''.<ref name="db" /> The Alliance Fleet eventually destroyed the second Death Star after the superweapon's shields were finally disabled by the ground team on Endor, winning the battle against the Empire.<ref name="rotj" /> Following the Battle of Endor, the relatively undamaged ''Torktarak'' was returned to the Dornean Navy.<ref name="db" />

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Torktarak'' had a crew of seventy-five Dornean Navy personnel under the command of Etahn A'baht. After the battle, A'baht was promoted to [[General/Legends|general]], and he continued to serve for several more years in the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|military forces]] of the Rebel Alliance, which were reorganized into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] after Endor.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Battle_of_Endor.jpg|thumb|right|220px|The unidentified ''Braha'tok''-class gunship to the lower left of a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]], during the Battle of Endor]]
{{Quote|I'd envisioned 'Braha'tok' and 'Torktarak' as some sort of mythical reference, either to a pair of Dornean brothers, gods, or monsters, but I was unable to fit that in with the flow of the entry.|Ello137, on creating the ''Braha-tok''-class gunships|<ref name="blog" />}}
The ''Torktarak'' was created in [[2006]] in [[Ello137]]'s winning entry in the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature, "[[What's The Story?]]" The campaign allowed fans to provide the backstory for certain aspects of the saga; in Ello137's case, the ''Braha'tok''-class gunships. Originally, Ello137 intended the ship's name to be a mythical Dornean name but was unable to fit it into the "flow" of his final entry.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|apocalypselater|16/comments|What's the Story, Ello137? Funny you should ask …}}</ref> The information on the ''Torktarak'' from Ello137's entry was later used in the entry on the ''Braha'tok''-class gunship included in the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.

In [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], a ''Braha'tok''-class gunship appears in the background during the space battle over Endor. However, the gunship has never been identified as either the ''Torktarak'' or the ''Braha'tok.
